input.prompt: Explore the src/ directory structure of this codebase. Identify what type of project this is (language, framework). Return a brief summary: 1) what language(s) are used, 2) what framework if any, 3) what the main source directories appear to contain based on their names. Do not read full files, just analyze directory structure.

state.output:
task_id: ses_271fe3118ffe334ODIwTBRBwUo (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Based on the `src/` directory structure and project configuration, here's my analysis:

## Project Summary

**1. Language(s):** C++ (primary)

**2. Framework:** 
- **FTXUI** (version 6.1.9) - A Functional Terminal UI library for C++. This is a **TUI (Text User Interface)** application that runs in the terminal.
- Additional notable dependencies:
  - `cpp-httplib` - HTTP client/server library
  - `nlohmann_json` - JSON parsing
  - `yaml-cpp` - YAML parsing
  - `cmark-gfm` - GitHub-flavored Markdown parsing
  - Built with **Bazel** build system

**3. Main Source Directories:**

| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `src/app/` | Main application entry point, context, and navigation state |
| `src/auth/` | Authentication functionality |
| `src/commands/` | Command handlers (task, storage) |
| `src/components/` | Reusable UI components (splash, autocomplete, nav_bar, bottom_bar, health_monitor, scrollable, status_bar) |
| `src/configuration/` | Configuration management |
| `src/models/` | Data models (Task, Workflow, Result) |
| `src/opencode/` | Core opencode service, client, SSE handling, lifecycle |
| `src/providers/` | Data providers (artifact, workflow, repo_file) |
| `src/rendering/` | Rendering engines (TreeSitter renderer, Plain renderer, query store) |
| `src/services/` | Services including file watchers (Skills, Artifact watchers) |
| `src/style/` | Styling (colour palette, form styling) |
| `src/utils/` | Utility functions (time formatting, etc.) |
| `src/views/` | Screen views (board, create_task, create_thread, stage_view, task_detail, workflow_form, workflow_list) |
| `src/tools/` | Tooling |

This appears to be a **workflow/project management TUI application** with features for managing tasks, workflows, skills, artifacts, and possibly code-related operations. The "opencode" module suggests integration with some external service or API.
</task_result>

2: # Usage: just <recipe>
3: 
4: # List available recipes

7: 
8: # Format all C++ source files with clang-format
9: format:

11: 
12: # Run clang-tidy linter on all C++ source files

metadata.preview:
# Ally development workflows
# Usage: just <recipe>

# List available recipes
default:
    @just --justfile {{justfile()}} --list

# Format all C++ source files with clang-format
format:
    find src -name '*.cpp' -o -name '*.hpp' -o -name '*.h' -o -name '*.cc' | xargs clang-format -i

# Run clang-tidy linter on all C++ source files
lint:
    find src -name '*.cpp' | xargs clang-tidy -p .

# Run clang-tidy and auto-fix issues
lint-fix:
    find src -name '*.cpp' | xargs clang-tidy -p . --fix

# Build the project with Bazel

99:  private:
100:   explicit OpenCodeClient(std::string base_url);
101: 
102:   template <typename T>
103:   auto HandleResponse(const httplib::Result& res) -> Result<T>;
104:   static auto HandleEmptyResponse(const httplib::Result& res) -> Result<std::monostate>;
105: 
106:   std::unique_ptr<httplib::Client> client_;
107:   std::string base_url_;
108: };
109: 
110: }  // namespace ally::opencode

(End of file - total 110 lines)
</content>
